title: Software Update - DFU - Headset at low battery - Software update tab in device settings
scenario: DFU blocked with pop-up when headset battery is below 5%, but proceeds if headset is plugged into charger
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
2. Navigate to Home screen and ensure a Poly Bluetooth headset is connected.
3. Go to Device Settings → [Device Name] → Firmware Update.
4. Verify the headset battery level is below 5% (e.g., by checking the battery indicator on the Home screen or Device Settings).
5. Attempt to initiate a Device Firmware Update (DFU) while the headset battery is below 5% and not connected to a charger.
7. Connect the headset to a charger while the battery remains below 5%.
8. Attempt to initiate the DFU again.
10. While the update is in progress and the headset is still below 5% battery, unplug the headset from the charger.
expected: 1. Step 5-6: The app should display a pop-up with the message "Update currently not available, please try back later" and block the DFU. 2. Step 8-9: The DFU should proceed and complete successfully when the headset is plugged into a charger (even if battery is below 5%). 3. Step 10-11: After unplugging the charger during the update (while still below 5%), the app should again display the pop-up "Update currently not available, please try back later" and block/stop the update.
